In this paper, we propose an Inverse Graphics Capsule Network (IGC-Net) to learn the hierarchical 3D face representations from large-scale unlabeled images.
The core of IGC-Net is a new type of capsule, named graphics capsule.
which represents 3D primitives with interpretable parameters in computer graphics (CG), including depth, albedo, and 3D pose.

IGC-Net is proposed to learn the hierarchical 3D face representations from large-scale unlabeled in-the-wild images.
IGC-Net's primitive is the graphics capsule that contains the 3D representations with explicit meanings.
Combining depth and albedo information serves as cues for IGC-Net to decompose objects into part-level graphics capsules and construct the object hierarchy.
Part-level graphics capsules are assembled into object-level IGC-Net to demonstrate the hierarchy of objects.
Neural networks, oriented at visual perception, understand faces as a hierarchy of 3D models.
Part-level graphics descriptions can be used for unsupervised face segmentation and interpretable face analysis.
Experiments conducted on CelebA, BP4D, and Multi-PIE datasets validate the effectiveness and interpretability of the method.
